chunks.summary: File header, includes, private static env hash buffers (EnvNames, EnvValues, EnvCount, EnvSize), and five memory allocation wrapper functions: MemAllocExit (malloc+exit on fail), MemReallocExit (realloc+exit on fail), MemAllocReturn (malloc+return NULL on fail), MemReallocReturn (realloc+return NULL on fail), and MemFree (free with NULL guard).

String and buffer utility functions: ListMatch (exact string match in NULL-terminated list), ListNMatch (prefix match in list using strncmp), StringToupper (in-place uppercase conversion), BufConvert (byte-reversal of buffer), and GetCurrProgName (extract filename from path by stripping directory prefix).

Environment variable hash system: InitEnv allocates name/value hash buffer arrays (EnvNames/EnvValues) using MemAllocReturn. HashEnv iterates EnvTabPar (extern array of required env vars), first checking the hash cache, then calling getenv(), caching results with strdup, and growing buffers with MemReallocReturn. Returns ENV_PAR_MISS if any required variable is missing.

Core process lifecycle functions. InitSonProcess is the main child-process bootstrap: (1) InitEnv, (2) HashEnv, (3) OpenSemaphore, (4) OpenFirstExtent, (5) GetCurrProgName, (6) ShmGetParamsByName, (7) optional plock/mlockall, (8) setuid, (9) GetCurrNamedPipe, (10) ListenSocketNamed, (11) AddCurrProc, (12) AddToSonsCount. ExitSonProcess performs clean shutdown: DisposeSockets, DetachAllExtents, CloseSemaphore, exit().

BroadcastSonProcs broadcasts a message to all child processes by iterating PROC_TABLE, filtering by proc_flg (FIRST_SQL, NOT_CONTROL, JUST_CONTROL), connecting to each child's named pipe via ConnectSocketNamed with a 1-second SIGALRM timeout, and sending via WriteSocket. alarm_handler is a no-op SIGALRM handler used to break out of blocking syscalls.

GetCommonPars parses -r (retries) from command-line argv. GetFatherNamedPipe looks up FatherProcess named pipe path by iterating PROC_TABLE for FATHERPROC_TYPE. TssCanGoDown checks if a subsystem can shut down by counting active TSX25WORKPROC_TYPE workers in PROC_TABLE, with a throttle interval (GoDownCheckInterval).

Run_server forks and execs a child process. Extracts program name and arguments from proc_data->proc_name (whitespace-delimited), checks executable exists via fopen, collects additional arguments from PARM_TABLE (parameters matching prog_name.*), rotates the child's log file, and forks. Child calls execv with assembled args array. Parent stores child pid in proc_data. Uses BinDir for executable path.

System utility functions: GetSystemLoad runs sar via popen and parses CPU load percentage. my_nap sleeps for milliseconds using select() on empty fd_set. run_cmd executes a shell command via popen with 5-second SIGALRM timeout. GetWord extracts next whitespace-delimited word from string. ToGoDown checks if system/subsystem status is GOING_DOWN by reading STAT_TABLE, with GoDownCheckInterval throttle.

Hebrew text encoding functions. buf_reverse reverses bytes in a buffer. fix_char swaps parentheses for RTL text. EncodeHebrew (added 26Nov2025) is a flexible encoding dispatcher: 'W' to 'D' delegates to WinHebToDosHeb, 'W' to 'U' delegates to WinHebToUTF8 with buffer size management. WinHebToDosHeb converts Windows-1255 Hebrew to DOS code page 862 by reversing Hebrew text segments, re-reversing embedded numbers, and subtracting 96 from byte values. WinHebToUTF8 converts Windows-1255 to UTF-8 using a static 128-entry translation table initialized on first call, with dynamic output buffer allocation via realloc.

Date arithmetic and conversion utilities. GetMonsDif calculates months difference between two YYYYMMDD dates with leap-year awareness. GetDaysDiff calculates days difference using mktime. AddDays adds N days to YYYYMMDD date. AddMonths adds N months with month-length correction. AddSeconds adds N seconds to HHMMSS time. ConvertUnitAmount converts medication amounts between units (mcg/MIU*0.001, g*1000 to mg). ParseISODateTime (added 2025-04-22 by Marianna) parses ISO 8601 datetime to YYYYMMDD + HHMMSS integers.

File header with MESG_HEADER struct (length + mesg_type) and debug macros. ListenSocketNamed creates AF_UNIX/SOCK_STREAM socket, binds to path, listens with SOMAXCONN backlog, stores in global NamedPipeSocket. ConnectSocketNamed creates AF_UNIX socket and connects to a named path, with EINTR detection for alarm-interrupted connects.

TCP/IP (AF_INET) socket functions. ListenSocketUnnamed creates, binds, and listens on a TCP socket with SO_REUSEADDR. ConnectSocketUnnamed creates an AF_INET socket and connects to a remote address:port. Both follow the same pattern as their Named counterparts but use struct sockaddr_in.

Socket lifecycle management. AcceptConnection wraps accept() with error handling. CloseSocket wraps close() with a guard against closing fd < 3 (stdin/stdout/stderr). DisposeSockets unlinks the named pipe file (CurrNamedPipe) for cleanup.

Framed message I/O. ReadSocket reads a MESSAGE_HEADER (type+length) via get_header_details, then reads the payload via read_socket_data, handling overflow by discarding excess data. WriteSocket sends MESSAGE_HEADER via set_header_details then payload via write_socket_data. WriteSocketHead prepends a status byte before the payload. All use the MESSAGE_HEADER wire protocol with ASCII-formatted type and length fields.

Low-level socket I/O with select() timeouts. read_socket_data loops reading from socket with select() timeout (ReadTimeout global), handling partial reads, EINTR, and logging errors. write_socket_data loops writing with select() timeout (WriteTimeout global). get_header_details (static) reads MESSAGE_HEADER struct and parses messageType/messageLen via atoi. set_header_details formats type/length with sprintf into MESSAGE_HEADER and sends it.

High-level IPC API. GetPeerAddr returns dotted-decimal peer address. GetCurrNamedPipe builds per-process socket path: {NamedpDir}/{NAMEDP_PREFIX}{pid}_{time}. GetSocketMessage is the select()-based multiplexed message receiver on NamedPipeSocket with configurable timeout, accepts connections, reads framed messages, optionally leaves socket open (LEAVE_OPEN_SOCK). GetInterProcMesg decodes inter-process messages: FILE_MESG reads data from a temp file, DATA_MESG copies inline data. SetInterProcMesg encodes messages: FILE_MESG writes to a temp file in NamedpDir named with pid+timestamp, DATA_MESG copies inline.

File header, includes (Global.h, CCGlobal.h, MsgHndlr.h), private statics: master_shm_header pointer, EXTENT_ADDR struct and ExtentChain array, ShmMode (0777), ShmKey1 (0x000abcde), AttachedExtentCount, ParametersRevision, ConnTab[CONN_MAX=150], ConnCount, extern TableTab[]. Key macros: RETURN(a) (release semaphore then return), VALIDITY_CHECK, MAKE_NULL_EXT_PTR, IS_NULL_EXT_PTR, GET_ABS_ADDR (resolves EXTENT_PTR to absolute address via ExtentChain[ext_no].start_addr + offset), CHECK_TAB_PTR (validates table connection), GET_FREE_EXTENT (finds free extent or calls AddNextExtent), ATTACH_ALL.

Shared memory extent lifecycle. InitFirstExtent (Father only): acquires semaphore, removes pre-existing shm via shmctl IPC_RMID, creates new segment with shmget(ShmKey1, shm_size, 0777|IPC_CREAT), initializes MASTER_SHM_HEADER and EXTENT_HEADER, allocates ExtentChain array (40 slots), initializes ConnTab. AddNextExtent (static, Father only): allocates additional shm extent, chains it, updates master header. KillAllExtents (Father, shutdown): detaches and IPC_RMID all extents. OpenFirstExtent (child processes): attaches to existing shm via shmget(ShmKey1,0,0)+shmat, initializes local ExtentChain and ConnTab. AttachAllExtents (static): attaches newly-allocated extents not yet attached locally. DetachAllExtents: detaches all attached extents. AttachShMem (static): low-level shmat wrapper.

Table CRUD operations. CreateTable (Father only): looks up schema in TableTab[], checks for duplicate names, allocates TABLE_HEADER in free extent, links into master table chain, calls OpenTable. OpenTable (extern C): finds table by name walking the table chain, allocates ConnTab entry, returns TABLE connection handle. CloseTable: releases ConnTab entry. AddItem: reuses free-chain row or allocates new space from extent, word-aligns record size, appends to row chain, copies data via memcpy. DeleteItem: moves row to free chain, updates doubly-linked list pointers. DeleteTable: bulk-deletes all rows (moves entire chain to free list). ListTables: debug print of all table names and row counts.

Row comparators and iteration engine. ParmComp, ProcComp, UpdtComp: compare rows for PARM_TABLE (by par_key), PROC_TABLE (by pid), UPDT_TABLE (by table_name). ActItems: core iteration engine walks row chain calling a function per row; supports OPER_FORWARD, OPER_STOP, OPER_BACKWARD return codes; returns NO_MORE_ROWS when chain exhausted. SetRecordSize: inflates table record size by 2.5x. RewindTable: resets cursor to first row. GetItem: copies current row data to caller's buffer. SeqFindItem: sequential key-range search operation. SetItem: overwrites current row data. GetProc: finds PROC_DATA row by pid. get_sys_status: reads system status from STAT_TABLE.

Process registration and statistics. AddCurrProc registers current process in PROC_TABLE with pid, name, type, system, named pipe, retries, and timestamp. UpdateTimeStat updates time-based statistics (pharmacy or doctor subsystem). IncrementCounters (operation): increments pharmacy statistics counters in TSTT_DATA for message processing times. IncrementDoctorCounters (operation): same for doctor statistics in DSTT_DATA. RescaleTimeStat zeroes stale time stat entries. RescaleCounters and RescaleDoctorCounters are the respective rescaling operations. GetMessageIndex maps message number to array index.

Parameter and SQL server management. IncrementRevision (operation): increments STAT_DATA.param_rev. UpdateShmParams: checks if parameter revision changed, if so reloads via ShmGetParamsByName(SonProcParams, PAR_RELOAD); throttled by SharedMemUpdateInterval. GetFreeSqlServer: iterates PROC_TABLE to find and lock an idle SQL server process matching proc_type. LockSqlServer (operation): atomically locks a free SQLPROC_TYPE entry by setting busy_flg. SetFreeSqlServer: releases a locked SQL server by pid. ReleaseSqlServer (operation): clears busy_flg for matching pid. ShmGetParamsByName: loads/reloads parameters from PARM_TABLE into a ParamTab descriptor array, handling PAR_INT/PAR_LONG/PAR_DOUBLE/PAR_CHAR types.

Miscellaneous shared memory operations. GetPrescriptionId: atomically reads and increments prescription ID from shm. GetMessageRecId: atomically reads and increments message rec_id. DiprComp: row comparator for DIPR_TABLE (by pid, then status). GetSonsCount: reads sons_count from STAT_TABLE. AddToSonsCount: atomically adds to sons_count with semaphore protection. GetTabNumByName: looks up table number in TableTab[] by name. UpdateLastAccessTime + UpdateLastAccessTimeInternal: update current process's last-access timestamp in PROC_TABLE. UpdateTable + GetTable: operations to update/get UPDT_DATA rows by table name match. set_sys_status: sets system/subsystem status in STAT_TABLE (locks shm, reads/modifies/writes STAT_DATA).

Complete semaphore facility with three compile-time implementations (XENIX, POSIX, BSD); only BSD is active (#define SEMAPHORES BSD_SEM). Statics: SemMode=0777, SemKey1=0x000abcde, sem_num, sem_busy_flg, sembuf structs W={0,-1,SEM_UNDO}, P={0,+1,SEM_UNDO}, WNB, PNB. CreateSemaphore (Father only): semget+semctl(SETVAL,1). DeleteSemaphore: semctl(IPC_RMID). OpenSemaphore (child): semget(IPC_CREAT) to access existing semaphore. CloseSemaphore: marks unused locally (sets sem_num=-1). WaitForResource: re-entrant locking via sem_busy_flg counter; if already held increments counter and returns, otherwise semop(W). ReleaseResource: decrements sem_busy_flg; only does semop(P) when counter reaches 0.

Error handling, debug helper, and build system. GeneralError.c provides the logging API: ErrorFmt and TitleFmt format strings, GerrLogExit (log+exit), GerrLogReturn (log+250ms usleep doom-loop protection+return), GerrLogMini (one-liner log), GerrLogAddBlankLines, GerrLogFnameMini, GerrLogFnameClear, GerrLogToFileName (log to named file+250ms delay), GmsgLogExit (titled log+exit), GmsgLogReturn (titled log+return). Internal helpers: open_log_file (opens {LogDir}/{LogFile} for append, falls back to stderr), close_log_file (flush+close unless stderr), open_other_file (opens {LogDir}/{fname}, creates directory if needed), create_log_directory (mkdir -m777 -p). GxxPersonality.c contains Prn() debug print helper (formats with pid/ppid prefix) and a #if 0'd __gxx_personality_v0 C++ exception stub. Makefile builds libgenlib.a from all 6 source files via $(CC) $(COMP_OPTS) + $(AR) r.
